_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: Widen mutmut mutation-testing scope from orch/daemon/ to orch/, second spike, flip mutation gate to blocking

steps.description: Backend: fix the pytest cov-fail-under interaction that produced 0 mutants in CR-00059 (pass --cov-fail-under=0 in the mutmut runner string in pyproject.toml + Makefile, OR add a mutmut-specific pyproject override and verify it applies). Widen pyproject.toml [tool.mutmut].paths_to_mutate from 'orch/daemon/' to 'orch/'. Update the Makefile mutation-audit loop to iterate orch/**/*.py (excluding __init__.py, __pycache__, and orch/db/migrations/). Extend tests/unit/test_mutmut_setup.py to assert the new 'orch/' scope (RED-first: change the assertion FIRST, capture AssertionError as tdd_red_evidence, then widen pyproject.toml). Run `make mutation-audit` end-to-end (DO NOT pre-prime with make test-unit / make test-integration — those duplicate QV gates S10/S11 and do not populate .mutmut-cache, which is mutmut-owned). Write the measurement file at ai-dev/active/CR-00080/evidences/pre/cr-00080-spike-measurements.txt with total wall-clock, mutants generated/killed/surviving, mutation score, per-module breakdown. If the audit exceeds 3600s, report completion_status=partial with the partial wall-clock and a [PARTIAL — terminated at <wall-clock>] prefix in the evidence file — S02's viability guard handles thin data cleanly. Finally rewrite the comment block above [tool.mutmut] to reference CR-00080 + the spike numbers. Pre-flight gates (make format / typecheck / lint) must pass. Targeted test verification only: `uv run pytest tests/unit/test_mutmut_setup.py -v`; do NOT run make test-unit or make test-integration as verification (S10 + S11 are the QV gates).

Backend: read the spike measurements from ai-dev/active/CR-00080/evidences/pre/cr-00080-spike-measurements.txt. Apply AC3 viability guard FIRST: parse M (mutation score) and K (killed + survived). If M < 20% OR K < 30, STOP — report completion_status=blocked with the measured M, K, and a recommended next step (expand test coverage in the most-mutated modules, or re-run the spike with a longer manual budget). DO NOT create the workflow file in this case. If viable, compute T = round_down(M) - margin where margin = 5 for M>=70, 3 for 50<=M<70, 2 for 20<=M<50. Then create .github/workflows/mutation.yml: schedule cron '0 6 * * *' + workflow_dispatch:{}, ubuntu-latest, actions/checkout@v4, install uv (official action), `uv sync --frozen`, `make mutation-audit | tee mutation-output.txt`, parse mutation score from the output, exit 1 if score<T, upload mutation-output.txt as an artifact. Set `permissions: contents: read`. NO continue-on-error, NO '|| true', NO `if: failure()` swallowing. Embed T and the ratchet rule as a top-of-file comment. Document M, K, T, and the viability outcome in the step report. Pre-flight gates pass. Test verification: YAML-validate the workflow file (`python -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/mutation.yml'))"`). tdd_red_evidence: 'n/a — gate-wiring + workflow YAML, no production logic'.

Backend: bring docs / tracker / skill into agreement with the gate state. Update docs/IW_AI_Core_Testing_Strategy.md §5 (gate table row for mutmut → blocking nightly GH workflow, T=<N>%), §8 (rewrite the spike narrative — fix landed, scope widened, second-spike W/M/K numbers, nightly surface + threshold + ratchet rule + viability guard), §9 (close the gap row). Update ai-dev/work/TESTS_ENHANCEMENT.md §5 row P2-CR-A-followup-mutation-block → DONE (CR-00080), §6 item 2.1 → DONE (CR-00080), §8 item 4.8 → DONE (CR-00080), §9 gate matrix mutmut row updated, §10 mutation-cost question answered citing W + per-mutant cost, §11 changelog dated 2026-05-24. Update skills/iw-ai-core-testing/SKILL.md mutmut section (widened scope, blocking nightly gate, threshold, preserve historical 'Earlier behaviour (CR-00059)' breadcrumb per CR-00049 precedent). Run `uv run iw sync-skills --force iw-ai-core-testing`; verify byte-equality via `diff skills/iw-ai-core-testing/SKILL.md .claude/skills/iw-ai-core-testing/SKILL.md` (must be empty). DO NOT touch skills/iw-workflow/SKILL.md — the canonical QV chain is unchanged. Cross-check: 'nightly GH workflow' phrasing and T value appear identically across strategy doc, tracker, skill, and S02's workflow YAML (grep). Pre-flight gates pass. tdd_red_evidence: 'n/a — documentation + tracker + skill updates only'. If S02 reported blocked, S03 still runs (documents the blocked state in tracker §10 + skill mutmut section: 'Gate wiring deferred until viability guard passes; spike data: M=<value>%, K=<value>; next step: <S02's recommendation>'); strategy doc / tracker §5/§6/§8 stay IN PROGRESS in that case, NOT DONE.

Per-agent review of S01 + S02 + S03 combined. AC1: cov-fail-under fix landed (runner string passes --cov-fail-under=0); paths_to_mutate = 'orch/'; Makefile audit loop iterates orch/**/*.py excluding migrations; guard test updated with plausible RED evidence; evidence file present and non-empty with required fields. AC2: .github/workflows/mutation.yml exists (if S02 wasn't blocked), has schedule + workflow_dispatch triggers, is blocking (no continue-on-error, no '|| true', no `if: failure()` swallowing), AND skills/iw-workflow/SKILL.md is UNCHANGED (the canonical QV chain must not be modified). AC3: viability guard correctly applied — if M < 20% OR K < 30, S02 status MUST be blocked and the workflow file MUST NOT exist. If viable, T = round_down(M) - margin (5/3/2 per band, with 20<=M<50 band) — verify by reading M from evidence and T from workflow YAML. Skill sync byte-equality verified by diff (iw-ai-core-testing only). Scope check: git diff origin/main..HEAD names only files in allowed_paths; zero files under orch/ / dashboard/ / executor/ / orch/db/migrations/; SPECIFICALLY zero diff on skills/iw-workflow/SKILL.md or its project copy. Cross-surface consistency: T value identical across strategy doc, tracker, skill, workflow YAML. RED audit: S01 tdd_red_evidence is AssertionError (not ImportError/collection error); S02 + S03 use 'n/a — …'. Pre-review gate (`make lint` + `make format-check`) must show zero new violations. Targeted test verification: `uv run pytest tests/unit/test_mutmut_setup.py -v`. Verdict pass iff zero CRITICAL + zero HIGH + zero MEDIUM(fixable).

Global cross-agent review of S01..S04. End-to-end AC1..AC5 verification with actual file reads (not just trusting step reports). Confirm skills/iw-workflow/SKILL.md was NOT modified (canonical QV chain is unchanged — mutmut lives on the nightly surface only). No production code outside scope. No new dependencies in pyproject.toml dependencies array. Cross-surface grep: T integer appears identically across strategy doc / tracker / skill / .github/workflows/mutation.yml. Tracker has zero remaining IN PROGRESS or TODO references to 'P2-CR-A-followup-mutation-block' / 'item 2.1' / 'item 4.8' / 'Tighten mutation gate to blocking' (UNLESS S02 was blocked by the viability guard, in which case those entries stay IN PROGRESS with a §10 / §11 note explaining why). Viability guard audit: if M < 20% OR K < 30 in the evidence file, S02 status MUST be blocked, .github/workflows/mutation.yml MUST NOT exist, and the tracker / strategy doc MUST reflect the deferred state. Run `uv run pytest tests/unit/test_mutmut_setup.py -v`. make quality runs (warn-only steps don't fail). make check NOT run here — that's S06–S13.
QV: Assertion scanner (forbid new vacuous tests)
QV: Unit tests (includes the widened test_mutmut_setup.py assertion)
QV: Diff coverage (new/changed lines must be well-covered)

Self-assessment of the just-completed item via the iw-item-analyze skill. CR-00080-specific focus: did S01 hit the 3600s budget? did the cov-fail-under fix actually produce >0 mutants? did the viability guard fire (M<20% OR K<30) and was it handled correctly (blocked, no workflow file, deferred tracker state)? did the threshold formula land in the correct band (5/3/2 margin)? did skill sync work first try (iw-ai-core-testing only)? was step-granularity respected (S01 spike vs S02 wiring vs S03 docs)? did S04 catch any T-inconsistency across the four surfaces or did S05 have to catch it? canonical-chain audit: did the canonical QV chain in skills/iw-workflow/SKILL.md stay UNCHANGED (it must — mutmut lives on the nightly surface only)?
